behavior_summary: INTERG::hatch is the handler for a 'U8P_INTERG::hatch' fast-area event. It acts as a guarded trigger that may initiate an item-use action for a target process and then yield/suspend.

On entry it checks three global flags (benticCacked, interrogEW, devonInRule) and returns immediately if the specific combination benticCacked == 1 AND interrogEW == 0 AND devonInRule == 0 holds. This combination acts as a guard preventing the U8P_INTERG::hatch action.

If the guard does not match, the handler pushes a pid and invokes Item::use with a small action code (0x3B) and a stack pointer for extra args/results. It processes the returned value (an 'implies' operation and pop) and then suspends, indicating the use was initiated and the script yields.
notable_control_flow: Early-exit guard: immediate return when (benticCacked == 1) AND (interrogEW == 0) AND (devonInRule == 0).
If guard fails, call to external Item::use is performed with parameters (pid, 0x3B, addr), the return value is post-processed (implies/pop), and the function suspends.
No globals are written by this handler; it is primarily a conditional trigger that delegates the action to Item::use.
open_questions: What in-game meaning do the globals benticCacked, interrogEW, and devonInRule encode (quest states, one-time guards, or mode flags)?
What exactly is the action code 0x3B passed to Item::use — a specific U8P_INTERG::hatch animation, spawn, or scripted interaction?
Is BP+06h the PID of the triggering actor, the item instance that should perform the use, or a context object? The disassembly implies a pid is pushed but the exact origin is inferred.
Is the handler invoked on area-enter only (enterFastArea) or also on area-leave; i.e., does Event 7 represent a single U8P_INTERG::hatch moment or repeated triggers?

main_function.name: U8P_INTERG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Serve as the 'U8P_INTERG::hatch' fast-area event handler: check guard flags and, unless blocked, request an Item::use action (action code 0x3B) for a target pid, process the return value, and suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 7 (fast-area 'U8P_INTERG::hatch' trigger) — invoked when the associated area/event fires.
Runs only when the early-exit guard (benticCacked == 1 && interrogEW == 0 && devonInRule == 0) is false; otherwise it returns immediately.

function_map.name: U8P_INTERG::hatch
function_map.role: U8P_INTERG::hatch event handler for the INTERG object; guards and delegates a U8P_INTERG::hatch action to Item::use

external_calls.purpose: Trigger an item-use/action for a target process/pid using action code 0x3B and an address for extra args/results. Used to perform the U8P_INTERG::hatch-related behavior when guard does not block it.

reads.name: benticCacked
reads.likely_effect: Part of a conjunctive guard; when == 1 contributes to blocking the U8P_INTERG::hatch action. Appears to be a one-bit/boolean state used as a guard.

reads.name: interrogEW
reads.likely_effect: Part of the same guard; checked for == 0 to contribute to blocking the U8P_INTERG::hatch action. Likely encodes a complementary state to benticCacked.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Also part of the guard; checked for == 0 as part of the conjunction that prevents the U8P_INTERG::hatch action when combined with benticCacked.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: INTERG::hatch is the handler for a 'U8P_INTERG::hatch' fast-area event. It acts as a guarded trigger that may initiate an item-use action for a target process and then yield/suspend.
